perturbation.original_text: THIS AGREEMENT SHALL BE CONSTRUED, ENFORCED, AND GOVERNED BY THE INTERNAL LAWS OF THE STATE OF DELAWARE (WITHOUT REGARD TO ITS CHOICE OF LAW PRINCIPLES).
perturbation.changed_text: THIS AGREEMENT SHALL BE CONSTRUED, ENFORCED, AND GOVERNED BY THE INTERNAL LAWS OF THE STATE OF DELAWARE (WITHOUT REGARD TO ITS CHOICE OF LAW PRINCIPLES). Samples acknowledges and agrees that each of the covenants contained herein is a reasonable limitation as to time, geographical area and scope of activity to be restrained and does not impose a greater restraint than is necessary to protect the goodwill or other interests of the Acquiror Companies and the Target Company and the Related Entities.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the reasonableness clause of the non-compete agreement into the governing law section makes it harder to locate and understand in the context of restrictive covenants, potentially impacting its enforceability.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Delaware law requires that non-compete agreements be reasonable in scope and duration to be enforceable, and this reasonableness must be clearly demonstrable.
perturbation.law_explanation: Delaware courts assess the reasonableness of non-compete agreements by considering factors like geographic scope, duration, and the nature of the business interest being protected. Placing the acknowledgment of reasonableness in an unrelated section like 'Governing Law' obfuscates its connection to the actual restrictive covenants, making it more difficult to argue that the employee fully understood and agreed to the restrictions, undermining the enforceability under Delaware law.

Contracts Subchapter I. General Provisions Â§ 2701. Joint and several contracts. An obligation or written contract of several persons shall be joint and several, unless otherwise expressed. Code 1852, Â§ â1170;Â Code 1915, Â§ â2628;Â Code 1935, Â§ â3108;Â 6 Del. C. 1953, Â§ â2701;Â Â§ 2702. Assignment of bonds, specialties and notes. (a) All bonds, specialties, and notes in writing, payable to any person, or order, or assigns, may be assigned, or indorsed, and the assignee, or indorsee, his or her executors, administrators, or assigns, may again assign, or indorse the same, as often as desired. The assignees, or indorsees, or their executors, or administrators, may, in their own name, sue for and recover the money due on the bonds, specialties, or notes. All assignments of bonds or specialties shall be under hand and seal, and executed before at least one credible witness. (b) Assignors, or indorsers, or their executors, or administrators may not release or discharge any sum due by the bonds, specialties, or notes, after the date of the assignment, and no release, receipt, or discharge from him, her or them made after the date of the assignment shall be good or available. perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a structural flaw by embedding the reasonableness clause, typically associated with non-compete agreements, within the 'Governing Law' section. This obfuscates its relevance to the actual restrictive covenants, potentially undermining its enforceability under Delaware law, as indicated by the Law Explanation. While Scraped Law Snippet 1 doesn't directly address non-compete agreements, the Law Explanation states that Delaware courts assess the reasonableness of such agreements, which is made more difficult by this structural flaw, as the clause is harder to locate and understand, creating a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Samples recognizes and agrees that, on and after the date hereof, he will not have the right to use for his own account any of the service marks, trademarks, trade names, licenses, labels, trade secrets or customers' lists owned by or licensed to the Acquiror Companies, the Target Company or any of the Related Entities.
perturbation.changed_text: Samples recognizes and agrees that, on and after the date hereof, he will not have the right to use for his own account any of the service marks, trademarks, trade names, licenses, labels, trade secrets or customers' lists owned by or licensed to the Acquiror Companies, the Target Company or any of the Related Entities. Any notice, request, instruction, document or other communication to be given hereunder by any party hereto to any other party hereto shall be in writing and validly given if (i) delivered personally, (ii) sent by telecopy, (iii) delivered by overnight express, or (iv) sent by registered or certified mail, postage prepaid, as follows:
perturbation.explanation: Moving part of the 'Notice' section to immediately after the 'Intellectual Property' section disrupts the logical flow of the contract. The notice provision becomes harder to find when needed, reducing its practical enforceability.
perturbation.contradicted_law: While not directly violating a specific statute, this placement violates the principle of good faith and fair dealing implied in contracts.
perturbation.law_explanation: By inserting the beginning of the 'Notice' clause into a section discussing intellectual property, this significantly disrupts the flow and understanding of the document and the ability to easily notify the other party when needed. The implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing requires parties to act honestly and reasonably in the performance and enforcement of contracts. This alteration impairs the practical usefulness of the 'Notice' section, potentially hindering communication and dispute resolution, therefore running contrary to principles of good faith and fair dealing.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: good faith | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you good faith Good faith is a broad term that’s used to encompass honest dealing. Depending on the exact setting, good faith may require an honest belief or purpose, faithful performance of duties , observance of fair dealing standards, or an absence of fraudulent intent . A fiduciary relationship creates a duty of good faith between the agent and the principal . The breach of this duty of good faith can lead to liability . Failure to act in good faith is known as bad faith and is generally considered to be a level of culpability greater than negligence . [Last reviewed in January of 2023 by the Wex Definitions Team ] Wex COMMERCE business law contracts wex definitions business sectors legal education and practice Wex Toolbox
perturbation.justification: The perturbation introduces a structural flaw by disrupting the logical flow of the contract, specifically by inserting part of the 'Notice' section into the 'Intellectual Property' section. The 'Law Explanation' suggests that this disruption could impair the practical usefulness of the 'Notice' section, potentially hindering communication and dispute resolution, which runs contrary to principles of good faith and fair dealing. Scraped Law Snippet 1 defines good faith as encompassing 'honest dealing' and requiring 'faithful performance of duties' and 'observance of fair dealing standards.' By making it harder to find and understand the notice provision, the modified text arguably undermines the 'honest dealing' and 'fair dealing standards' expected in contract performance, suggesting a contradiction.
